'''''UFC 324: Gaethje vs. Pimblett''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on January 24, 2026, at the [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas Valley]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/ufc/news/ufc-324-fight-card-justin-gaethje-paddy-pimblett-kayla-harrison-amanda-nunes-paramount|title=UFC 324: Gaethje vs. Pimblett, Kayla Harrison vs. Amanda Nunes to headline Paramount+ debut card|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Shakiel Mahjouri|date=2025-11-27|access-date=2025-11-27}}</ref>

==Background==
This event was the first UFC broadcast under the new media rights agreement with [[Paramount Skydance]], following the conclusion of their deal with [[ESPN]]. The deal has already expanded to include Latin America and Australia, as all the events will be available to stream at no additional cost on [[Paramount+]] (including numbered events), with select cards appearing on CBS.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/international-sports/dana-whites-long-term-plan-surfaces-as-paramount-plus-deal-launches-with-early-2026-fight-announcement-for-ufc-324/articleshow/125424964.cms|title=Dana White’s long-term plan surfaces as Paramount Plus deal launches with early 2026 fight announcement for UFC 324|publisher=timesofindia.indiatimes.com|author=TOI Sports Desk|date=2025-11-19|access-date=2025-11-19}}</ref> It also marked the first numbered event to not air on [[pay-per-view]] in the United States since [[UFC 267]] in 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2021/07/24/ufc-267-feature-double-header-non-ppv-united-states/|title=UFC 267 targeted as double-header, non-PPV event|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Gabriel Gonzalez|date=2021-07-24|access-date=2025-11-28}}</ref>

An interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout between former interim champion (also former [[WSOF Lightweight Championship|WSOF Lightweight Champion]]) [[Justin Gaethje]] and [[Paddy Pimblett]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/11/27/ufc-324-justin-gaethje-vs-paddy-pimblett-booked-interim-lightweight-title/87487421007/|title=Justin Gaethje vs. Paddy Pimblett headlines UFC 324 for interim title|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells and Nolan King|date=2025-11-27|access-date=2025-11-27}}</ref> Current champion (also former [[UFC Featherweight Champion]]) [[Ilia Topuria]] announced that he would not be competing in the first quarter of 2026 due to personal reasons relating to his wife at the time.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/458811/ilia-topuria-releases-statement-declaring-he-wont-fight-in-first-quarter-of-2026|title=Ilia Topuria releases statement declaring he won’t fight in first quarter of 2026|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2025-11-28|access-date=2025-11-28}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://athlonsports.com/mma/ufc-ilia-topuria-opens-up-about-divorce-first-time|title=UFC lightweight champion Ilia Topuria has finally publicly addressed his divorce from ex-wife Giorgina Uzcategui for the first time|publisher=athlonsports.com|author=Michael Aldon|date=2026-03-16|access-date=2026-04-10}}</ref>

A [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] bout between the reigning champion [[Kayla Harrison]] (who is also a [[Judo at the 2012 Summer Olympics – Women's 78 kg|2012]] and [[Judo at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's 78 kg|2016 Olympic gold medalist in judo]]; and a two-time [[Professional Fighters League|PFL]] women's lightweight tournament champion) and former two-time champion [[Amanda Nunes]], who also held the [[UFC Women's Featherweight Championship]], was originally scheduled to serve as the co-main event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/11/27/ufc-324-kayla-harrison-vs-amanda-nunes-booked-paramount-debut/87487847007/|title=Kayla Harrison vs. Amanda Nunes title bout set for UFC 324 co-main|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells and Nolan King|date=2025-11-27|access-date=2025-11-27}}</ref> Nunes, who is widely considered the greatest women's fighter in UFC history, was supposed to return for her first bout since [[UFC 289]] in June 2023, where she announced her retirement following a successful title defense against [[Irene Aldana]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsnet.ca/ufc/article/amanda-nunes-returning-to-face-kayla-harrison-for-title-at-ufc-324|title=Amanda Nunes returning to face Kayla Harrison for title at UFC 324|publisher=sportsnet.ca|author=Mike Johnston|date=2025-11-27|access-date=2025-11-28}}</ref> However, Harrison withdrew from the bout after being diagnosed with herniated discs in her neck, which required surgery. The fight is expected to be rescheduled for a later date.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/466164/report-kayla-harrison-out-of-ufc-324-fight-against-amanda-nunes-postponed-to-later-date|title=Kayla Harrison out of UFC 324, fight against Amanda Nunes postponed to later date|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2026-01-14|access-date=2026-01-14}}</ref>

Former [[UFC Women's Flyweight Championship|UFC Women's Flyweight Champion]] [[Alexa Grasso]] and former two-time [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship|UFC Women's Strawweight Champion]] [[Rose Namajunas]] were expected to meet in a flyweight bout at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/11/27/ufc-324-fight-card-sean-omalley-song-yadong-rose-namajunas-alexa-grasso/87488327007/|title=UFC 324 fight card: Sean O'Malley returns, ex-women's champs collide|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells|date=2025-11-27|access-date=2025-11-27}}</ref> However, Grasso pulled out on December 3 due to injury and was replaced by [[Natália Silva (fighter)|Natália Silva]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Streaking-Flyweight-Contender-Steps-in-to-Face-Rose-Namajunas-at-UFC-324-199285|title=Streaking flyweight contender steps in to face Rose Namajunas at UFC 324|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Marcelo Alonso|date=2025-12-03|access-date=2025-12-03}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, two fighters missed weight:<ref name="weightmiss">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/01/23/ufc-324-justin-gaethje-vs-paddy-pimblett-interim-title-weigh-in-results-live-video-stream/88314933007|title=UFC 324 weigh-in results: Title fight set; fall, misses bring drama|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2026-01-23|access-date=2026-01-23}}</ref>
*Former two-time [[UFC Flyweight Championship|UFC Flyweight Champion]] [[Deiveson Figueiredo]] weighed in at 138.5 pounds, two and a half pounds over the bantamweight non-title fight limit.
*Former flyweight title challenger [[Alex Perez (fighter)|Alex Perez]] weighed in at 128.5 pounds, two and a half pounds over the flyweight non-title fight limit.

Figueiredo and Perez's bouts proceeded at catchweight. Both fighters were fined 25 percent of their individual purses which went to their opponents former [[UFC Bantamweight Championship]] challenger [[Umar Nurmagomedov]] and [[Charles Johnson (fighter)|Charles Johnson]], respectively.<ref name="weightmiss"/>

Also at the weigh-ins, Cameron Smotherman made weight for his bantamweight contest against [[The Return of The Ultimate Fighter: Team Volkanovski vs. Team Ortega]] bantamweight winner Ricky Turcios, but collapsed moments after stepping off the scale. The bout was subsequently canceled. It is not yet known if Turcios will be paid his win money, in addition to his show money, as he made weight as scheduled.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/01/23/ufc-324-cameron-smotherman-faint-weigh-ins-passes-out/88319220007|title=UFC 324 fighter Cameron Smotherman faints, faceplants on weigh-ins stage|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2026-01-23|access-date=2026-01-23}}</ref>

A lightweight bout between [[Michael Johnson (fighter)|Michael Johnson]] and [[Alexander Hernandez]] was originally scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/460440/ufc-324-lineup-revealed-for-justin-gaethje-vs-paddy-pimblett-fight-card|title=UFC 324: Lineup revealed for Justin Gaethje vs. Paddy Pimblett fight card|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2025-12-08|access-date=2025-12-08}}</ref> However, the fight was canceled on the day of the event, with the promotion giving no official reason. The cancellation followed notable betting‑line movement on the matchup, and it was reported that some observers speculated Hernandez may have been dealing with an injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bodyslam.net/2026/01/24/michael-johnson-vs-alexander-hernandez-called-off-from-ufc-324-card-promotion-announces/|title=Michael Johnson vs. Alexander Hernandez Called Off from UFC 324 Card, Promotion Announces|publisher=bodyslam.net|author=Rami Afif|date=2026-01-25|access-date=2026-01-25}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/ufc-324-news-results-paramount-streaming-updates-mma/416774/something-or-nothing-unusual-betting-activity-raises-questions-over-ufc-324-undercard-fight-gaethje-vs-pimblett-johnson-hernandez|title=Something or nothing? Unusual betting activity raises questions over UFC 324 undercard fight - Gaethje vs. Pimblett|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2026-01-24|access-date=2026-01-24}}</ref> UFC CEO and president [[Dana White]] later confirmed that the fight had been pulled from the card due to betting concerns.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/01/25/ufc-324-dana-white-alexander-hernandez-vs-michael-johnson/88346269007/|title=Dana White confirms UFC 324 fight was canceled due to betting concerns|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2026-01-25|access-date=2026-01-25}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, former two-time [[UFC Bantamweight Championship|UFC Bantamweight Champion]] (also former [[WEC Bantamweight Championship|WEC Bantamweight Champion]]) [[Dominick Cruz]] was announced as the next "modern wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee during International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas this summer.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thescore.com/s/34254115|title=Cruz to be inducted into UFC Hall of Fame|publisher=thescore.com|author=Nick Baldwin|date=2026-01-24|access-date=2026-01-24}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
This event marked the introduction of the promotion's updated bonus structure, which increased [[Bonus Awards|Performance of the Night]] and [[Bonus Awards|Fight of the Night]] awards from $50,000 to $100,000 and added additional $25,000 bonuses for knockouts or submissions not selected among the standard post‑fight awards.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/467628/ufc-doubling-post-fight-bonuses-from-50k-to-100k-introduces-new-finish-bonus|title=UFC doubling post-fight bonuses from $50K to $100K, introduces new finish bonus|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2026-01-25|access-date=2026-01-25}}</ref>

The following fighters received $100,000 bonuses. The other finishes received $25,000 additional bonuses, with the exception of Alex Perez, as he missed weight for his bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2026/01/25/ufc-324-main-event-snags-100k-fight-of-the-night-bonus/|title=UFC 324 main event snags 100k Fight of the Night bonus|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2026-01-25|access-date=2026-01-25}}</ref>

*'''Fight of the Night: Justin Gaethje vs. Paddy Pimblett'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Josh Hokit''' and '''Ty Miller'''
